content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`
Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

Its recommended schema includes run identity, namespace, `run_type`, `trigger_mode`, source and retrieved IDs, retrieval budget, prompt/model metadata, input/output ha...[truncated 202 chars] scope=Document section `02. Related tables `note_entities`, `note_s...[truncated 127 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Source as immutable evidence layer in v3 memory model summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Source` is the raw knowledge source for chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, and external records; it acts as the primary evidence layer for re-check and explainability. Its design emphasizes immutability, separate `observed_at` versus ingestion time, `content_hash` for dedupe, and an explicit delete policy. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`. keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy,dedupe,explainability tags=doc_digest,data_model,source,evidence_layer useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim schema and evidence linkage in v3 memory model summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Claim` is the atomic fact or relation-edge table used for factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval. Its schema tracks subject/predicate/object, normalized/context text, scope and qualifiers, confidence/importance, truth and retrieval states, temporal validity, supersession/retraction lineage, run lineage, embeddings, us...[truncated 154 chars] scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.5 `Claim`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, including recommended fields and the `claim_so...[truncated 13 chars] keywords=Claim,claim_sources,predicate reconciliation,explainable retrieval,support_type,evidence_quote tags=doc_digest,data_model,claim,evidence useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note invariants emphasize self-contained semantic fragments summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` invariants require one note to represent one semantic fragment, remain understandable without the original chat, add value beyond duplicating a source, treat `candidate_claims_json` only as hints, and allow a useful retrieval note to persist long-term without becoming a claim. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, specifically the Note invariants. keywords=Note invariants,semantic fragment,self-contained,candidate_claims_json,retrieval,claim tags=data_model,note_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note as intermediate semantic memory fragment summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment: not a `Source`, not a final `Claim`, and not chain-of-thought. It is meant for important, retrieval-useful context that should not yet be cemented as a hard fact, such as design directions, decision rationales, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, especially the definition and intended use case...[truncated 11 chars] keywords=Note,semantic memory fragment,Source,Claim,design direction,decision rationale,hypotheses,doc digests tags=data_model,note,semantic_memory useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity as canonical memory node with aliases and reversible merges summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims, tasks, notes, and episodes. It supports aliases and merge workflows, with invariants that merges do not delete history, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and erroneous merges must be reversible through an audit trail. scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=PredicateCatalog configures predicate reconciliation summary=In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `PredicateCatalog` is not a memory item but a reconciliation configuration that captures predicate-specific cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict behavior, projection sync flags, default importance, and active status. Its purpose is to handle predicates differently because some facts replace each other, some coexist, and some flow into profile or tas...[truncated 14 chars] scope=Document section `02. systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
